attributes.mlflow.spanOutputs: ["Florence Foster Jenkins | Florence Foster Jenkins (born Narcissa Florence Foster; July 19, 1868 – November 26, 1944) was an American socialite and amateur soprano who was known and mocked for her flamboyant performance costumes and notably poor singing ability. The historian Stephen Pile ranked her \"the world's worst opera singer\". \"No one, before or since,\" he wrote, \"has succeeded in liberating themselves quite so completely from the shackles of musical notation.\"", "Tarina Tarantino | Tarina Tarantino is an American costume jewelry and accessory designer, based in Los Angeles, California. Known for her flamboyant pink hair, she has been described as having \"a pretty cult-like following here in LA\" and as \"the haute designer of playful jewelry for grown women\".", "Emma Nevada | Emma Nevada (née Wixom) (7 February 1859 – 20 June 1940) was an American operatic soprano particularly known for her performances in operas by Bellini and Donizetti and the French composers Ambroise Thomas, Charles Gounod, and Léo Delibes. Considered one of the finest coloratura sopranos of the late 19th and early 20th centuries, her most famous roles were Amina in \"La sonnambula\", and the title roles in \"Lakmé\", \"Mignon\", \"Mireille\", and \"Lucia di Lammermoor."]

search_wikipedia

{"query": "American socialite amateur soprano flamboyant performance costumes"}

[[ ## observation_0 ## ]]
[1] «Florence Foster Jenkins | Florence Foster Jenkins (born Narcissa Florence Foster; July 19, 1868 – November 26, 1944) was an American socialite and amateur soprano who was known and mocked for her flamboyant performance costumes and notably poor singing ability. The historian Stephen Pile ranked her "the world's worst opera singer". "No one, before or since," he wrote, "has succeeded in liberating themselves quite so completely from the shackles of musical notation."»
[2] «Tarina Tarantino | Tarina Tarantino is an American costume jewelry and accessory designer, based in Los Angeles, California. Known for her flamboyant pink hair, she has been described as having "a pretty cult-like following here in LA" and as "the haute designer of playful jewelry for grown women".»
[3] «Emma Nevada | Emma Nevada (née Wixom) (7 February 1859 – 20 June 1940) was an American operatic soprano particularly known for her performances in operas by Bellini and Donizetti and the French composers Ambroise Thomas, Charles Gounod, and Léo Delibes. Considered one of the finest coloratura sopranos of the late 19th and early 20th centuries, her most famous roles were Amina in "La sonnambula", and the title roles in "Lakmé", "Mignon", "Mireille", and "Lucia di Lammermoor.»"}

    answer = Field(annotation=str required=True json_schema_extra={'__dspy_field_type': 'output', 'prefix': 'Answer:', 'desc': '${answer}'})
)", "completion": "[[ ## reasoning ## ]]
